nodes.label:
Soil
Humidity
nodes.desc: Current moisture level in the soil. High humidity means the soil is already saturated. It is then a bad thing to do nothing, and a good thing to water the plants.

nodes.label:
Insect
Infestation
nodes.desc: The presence of insects that feed on plants can significantly reduce their health and productivity.

nodes.label:
Water 
the plants
nodes.desc: Use tap water to maintain hydration. Essential for growth but impacts the water table and operational costs.

nodes.label: Do nothing
nodes.desc: Wait and observe. Saves all immediate resources but risks plant wilting and loss of potential income.

nodes.label:
Utilize chemical 
fertilizers
nodes.desc: Use chemical fertilizers to boost plant growth. May have negative environmental impacts.

nodes.label:
Water
Conservation
nodes.desc: Fresh water is a precious resource. Avoiding unnecessary usage is key to long-term environmental sustainability.

nodes.label:
Plant
Vitality
nodes.desc: Living organisms have a right to thrive. Allowing plants to die is a failure of the gardener's ecological duty.

nodes.label:
Soil & Water
Health
nodes.desc: Chemical fertilizers can lead to soil degradation and water pollution, harming local ecosystems.

nodes.label:
Maximize
Production
nodes.desc: Selling healthy flowers is the only way to sustain the business and allow for future investments.

nodes.label:
Minimize
Costs
nodes.desc: Profit is Revenue minus Costs. Keeping utility bills low is essential for a high profit margin.
